title: AI Engineer
employment_type: Full Time, Part Time, Freelance / Project, Hybrid
description: We are looking for an AI Automation & Integration Engineer to design, build, and maintain smart business automations that improve efficiency, reduce manual work, and connect our systems end-to-end. This role is ideal for someone who has hands-on experience building real automations in n8n, understands integrations deeply, and can combine AI, APIs, webhooks, and business logic into reliable workflows. This is not a theory role. We are looking for someone who has actually built and deployed n8n automations in production. Design, build, test, and maintain automation workflows using n8n Integrate business tools, internal systems, and third-party platforms through APIs, webhooks, and connectors Build AI-powered workflows using LLMs and related tools for tasks such as content generation, classification, research, routing, data extraction, and decision support Develop automations for CRM, lead management, notifications, approvals, reporting, customer operations, and internal productivity. Write and maintain custom logic using Java Script / Type Script when low-code steps are not enough Handle data mapping, transformation, validation, and synchronization across systems. Monitor workflow performance, troubleshoot errors, and improve reliability Document automations clearly so they are maintainable and scalable Collaborate remotely with stakeholders to understand business processes and convert them into practical automation solutions. Recommend the best architecture, tools, and workflow design standards to get maximum value from n8n.
requirements: Demonstrated experience in Automation Engineering and building automated systems. Proven ability to act as a Workflow Architect , optimizing complex processes. Strong background in implementing Business Process AI solutions. Expert-level skills in Python programming. Solid understanding and practical application of Machine Learning and Deep Learning algorithms. Proficiency in Data Science techniques and methodologies. Strong capabilities in System Design and architectural planning. Experience with Project Management principles and practices. Fundamental understanding of Data Structures and algorithms. A minimum of 2-3 years of proven, hands-on experience in AI engineering, automation development, or related IT/Software Development roles. Demonstrated success in delivering tangible results through AI and automation projects.

title: Lead AI Systems Architect & Automation Head
description: Job Title Lead AI Systems Architect & Full-Stack Automation Head (Salary in USD) Job Description The Mission: We are a highly profitable ($1.2M/year profit) US-based firm. We don’t hire for "tasks"—we hire Intelligence. We are looking for a Founding Engineer in Egypt to act as the sole architect of an ever-evolving ecosystem of AI-driven products. You will have a direct impact on reducing overhead and maximizing revenue through pure technical innovation. If you want to build the "Brain" of a multi-million dollar company, this is your seat. The Capabilities You Must Possess: We are looking for the top 0.1% of engineers in Egypt. You must be a "Generalist Specialist" capable of rapid prototyping and deployment across any domain we dream up: Autonomous Intelligence: Mastery of LLM Orchestration (GPT-4o/Claude), Vector Databases (Pinecone/Milvus), and RAG. You build systems that learn. Global Voice Infrastructure: Expertise in VoIP/SIP (Asterisk/Kamailio) and Real-time Audio (<600ms latency) to build AI Dialing Centers that bypass international regulations. Web Warfare: Elite-level Scraping and Anti-Bot evasion (Cloudflare, DataDome, JA3 fingerprinting) at high scale. Full-Stack Execution: Ability to build and deploy Web Apps, Mobile Apps, and Custom CRM integrations from scratch. Audio Engineering: Proficiency in Diarization (Whisper) and Machine Learning for analyzing sales/intake recordings. Compensation & Egypt-Specific Benefits: Base Salary: Top Salary USD / Month (Paid in USD via Deel/Elevate/Grey). This is a top-tier, stable salary protected from local inflation. Equity (Phantom Stock): 3% - 5% Stake. You aren't just an employee; you are a stakeholder. You own a piece of every system you build. Infrastructure Stipend: Monthly budget to guarantee your uptime (High-Speed Fiber + 5G Backup ISP + UPS/Power Redundancy for load shedding). The War Chest: Unlimited budget for premium proxies, AI tokens, and any hardware/servers required for the mission. Stability & Culture: 100% Remote, flexible Ramadan hours, and a long-term partnership with a profitable US firm.
requirements: TECHNICAL STACK REQUIREMENTS 1. The "Scraper & Lead Gen" Pillar (Data Acquisition) Advanced Browser Automation: Expert in Playwright or Puppeteer (Stealth-plugins are mandatory). Anti-Bot Evasion: Proven ability to bypass Cloudflare Turnstile, DataDome, and Akamai using JA3 fingerprinting , TLS header manipulation, and residential proxy rotation (Bright Data/Oxylabs). Asynchronous Processing: High-concurrency scraping using Python Asyncio or Go to pull thousands of leads without bottlenecking. 2. The "Voice & Dialing" Pillar (Infrastructure) VoIP/SIP Engineering: Deep experience with Asterisk, FreePBX, or Kamailio . Ability to manage SIP Trunks and DID (Phone Number) pools . Regulatory Compliance (US/Global): Knowledge of STIR/SHAKEN protocols and "A-Level Attestation" to prevent calls from being labeled "Scam Likely." Low-Latency Audio: Expertise in WebRTC and Vapi/Retell/Telnyx to keep AI voice response time under 500ms . • Server Management: Ability to deploy local or cloud-based "Gateway Servers" in specific regions to bypass regional AI dialing restrictions. 3. The "Training Brain" & RAG Pillar (Intelligence) Vector Databases: Implementation of Pinecone, Milvus, or Weaviate to store and retrieve successful sales call data. Multimodal RAG: Building systems that don't just "read" text but "analyze" audio files to identify winning sales patterns from top reps. Speech-to-Speech (S2S): Integration with GPT-4o Audio or ElevenLabs to create human-like voices with emotional intelligence (tone, laughter, pauses). Diarization & Transcription: Using OpenAI Whisper to split recordings into "Rep" vs. "Customer" for automated performance grading. 4. The "Automation & CRM" Pillar (Logic) Agentic Frameworks: Proficiency in LangGraph, CrewAI, or AutoGen to build "Agents" that can autonomously update the CRM, book appointments, and handle customer service. Airtable/CRM Mastery: Deep API integration with Airtable , HubSpot , or GoHighLevel using Webhooks and Python SDKs. Mobile App Integration: Ability to build a lightweight Flutter or React Native app interface for D2D guys to access the "Real-time Training Brain" in the field.

title: AI Engineer
description: Job Title: Generative AI Engineer (GenAI Engineer) Location: Remote / Hybrid (Flexible) Department: AI/ML Engineering Employment Type: Full-time Job Description We are looking for a talented Generative AI Engineer to design, build, and optimize production-grade generative AI applications. You will work on end-to-end AI solutions that combine large language models (LLMs), retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), agentic workflows, and multimodal capabilities to deliver intelligent, scalable, and reliable AI products. You will be a key member of a high-performing team building next-generation AI systems using modern frameworks and tools. Key Responsibilities Design, develop, and maintain production-ready GenAI applications using LangChain , LangGraph , and related orchestration frameworks. Build advanced RAG pipelines (including multi-stage retrieval, reranking, query transformation, and evaluation). Implement and optimize agentic workflows with LangGraph (stateful multi-agent systems, tool-calling, memory, human-in-the-loop). Work with vector databases (primarily Qdrant , also Pinecone, Weaviate, or Chroma) for efficient semantic search and retrieval. Fine-tune, quantize, and deploy LLMs and embedding models using PyTorch , Hugging Face Transformers, and PEFT methods (LoRA, QLoRA). Integrate and evaluate open-source and proprietary models (Llama 3, Mistral, Mixtral, GPT, Claude, Gemini, etc.). Implement evaluation frameworks (RAGAS, ARES, DeepEval, LangSmith) and monitoring for LLM applications. Optimize for cost, latency, and accuracy in production environments. Collaborate with backend, frontend, and data teams to integrate AI capabilities into user-facing products. Stay up-to-date with the latest advancements in LLMOps, agentic AI, and generative technologies. Technical Requirements (Must-Have) Strong proficiency in Python (advanced scripting, OOP, async programming, typing). Deep experience with LangChain & LangGraph (at least 1+ year of hands-on production use). Solid RAG expertise : advanced retrieval strategies, chunking, metadata filtering, hybrid search, reranking, and evaluation. Hands-on experience with Qdrant (or equivalent vector DBs) — collection management, filtering, hybrid search, payload optimization. PyTorch experience : model fine-tuning, LoRA/QLoRA, inference optimization (bitsandbytes, vLLM, TorchServe), custom training loops. Experience deploying LLMs (vLLM, TGI, Ollama, or cloud services like AWS Bedrock, Azure OpenAI, Vertex AI). Strong understanding of LLMOps (LangSmith, Phoenix, Helicone, Prometheus + Grafana). Experience with modern MLOps tools (Docker, Kubernetes, CI/CD, GitHub Actions).
requirements: Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, AI, Machine Learning, or equivalent experience. 3+ years of hands-on experience building production GenAI / LLM applications. Proven track record of deploying scalable RAG and agent systems. Strong software engineering fundamentals (clean code, testing, design patterns, API development — FastAPI preferred). Published projects, GitHub repositories, or contributions to open-source LangChain/LangGraph ecosystem.

title: Software Engineer (Technical Content)
employment_type: Full Time, Remote, CC Global Group, LLC
description: About Codecontent ‍ CodeContent is a UK-based technical content company founded by Mostafa Ibrahim, a former software engineer at GoCardless and Arm. We have published 300+ technical articles across 18 clients. We produce tutorials and deep technical blog posts for leading AI/ML platforms, YC-backed authentication companies, and major cloud infrastructure providers. Our team of 7 is made up of engineers who write, not writers who research. We have been operating for 3+ years. ‍ About the Role ‍ We are hiring a Software Engineer (Technical Content) to build and explain real software and AI systems for developer audiences. This is not a writing-only role. You will implement, run, and validate real code, then turn that work into long-form technical blogs and tutorials for our clients. You will work across a wide range of topics: system design, LLMs, RAG pipelines, agent-based workflows, APIs, cloud infrastructure, and developer tools. The output is written content, but the work is engineering.Your work will be published on the engineering blogs of leading AI/ML platforms, YC-backed startups, and major cloud infrastructure providers, reaching tens of thousands of developers worldwide. ‍ Responsibilities Write long-form technical blogs and tutorials based on provided briefsImplement, run, and validate real code as part of every article. Research and clearly explain software engineering and AI workflows to developer audiences. Cover topics including: Software engineering fundamentals and system design AI/ML concepts, LLMs, RAG systems, and agent-based workflows APIs, cloud infrastructure, and developer tools Revise drafts based on editorial and technical feedback Deliver consistently and on time ‍ How We Work ‍ This is a full-time position (40 hours/week) and your primary professional commitment. We are a small, focused team where every person carries significant ownership. ‍ ‍ Compensation EGP 30,000 – EGP 40,000 per month (based on experience) Direct mentorship from engineers with 300+ published pieces Fully remote Exposure to 10+ tech stacks annually Salary reviews every 6 months based on performance ‍ Why This Role ‍ This is a role for engineers who enjoy explaining how things work. You will spend your days building real systems and writing about them for technical audiences. No fluff, no marketing spin. You will gain deep experience writing production-grade technical content.
requirements: Required Qualifications ‍ Hands-on programming experience: you can implement and debug real code, not just describe it Experience working as a software engineer or in an equivalent technical role Ability to explain complex technical concepts clearly to developer audiences Comfort working with real-world tools, libraries, APIs, and infrastructure Strong written communication: You can write clearly, concisely, and accurately ‍ Nice to Have ‍ Experience with AI infrastructure, LLM tooling, or MLOps Familiarity with cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, Azure) and their APIs Kubernetes or distributed systems experience Previous exposure to developer-focused SaaS products or technical documentation A portfolio of published technical content (blogs, tutorials, docs)

title: Senior AI Engineer
description: About the Role We are seeking an engineer who doesn't just "talk" to LLMs but builds autonomous, resilient systems. You will design multi-agent architecture that can reason, use tools, and recover from failures independently. Your primary goal is to bridge the gap between "cool demos" and "production-grade reliability." You will be responsible for deploying agents across various business sectors (e.g., Finance, Operations, Customer Success), ensuring they are not only intelligent but also safe, cost-effective, and predictable in a production environment. Key Responsibilities ● Agent Orchestration: Design and implement stateful, multi-turn agent workflows using frameworks like LangGraph, CrewAI, AutoGen, PydanticAI, Swarm, Haystack, or Bee Agent Framework . ● Proactive Reliability & Guardrails: Architect systems that prevent production meltdowns. Implement circuit breakers, "human-in-the-loop" triggers, and input/output guardrails to stop infinite loops, prompt injections, and hallucinated tool calls before they reach the end user. ● Multi-Sector Tooling: Build and maintain high-precision API integrations (tools) that allow agents to interact with diverse business systems (ERPs, CRMs, custom databases) deterministically. ● Observability & Tracing: Set up advanced tracing (e.g., LangSmith, Langfuse, or Arize Phoenix ) to debug complex reasoning chains and monitor agent trajectories in real-time. ● Rigorous Evaluation (Evals): Develop automated "Golden Datasets" and evaluation frameworks (using RAGas, DeepEval, G-Eval, LangSmith Evaluators, or custom model-based grading ) to measure agent success rates and prevent regressions before every deployment. ● Cost & Latency Optimization: Manage the "token budget" by implementing tiered model routing (e.g., using Gemini 3 Flash for initial reasoning and Ultra for final verification) and optimizing context window usage.
requirements: Required Qualifications ● 3+ years of Software Engineering experience , with a background in Python and FastAPI (or similar). ● 1+ year of experience specifically focused on LLM-based Agents in a production environment. ● Deep Expertise in Agentic Patterns: Proven experience implementing ReAct, Plan-and-Execute, or Multi-Agent Supervisor architectures. ● Production Infrastructure: Hands-on experience with modern Vector Databases and Search Engines (e.g., Pinecone, Milvus, Weaviate, Qdrant, Chroma, or pgvector) and semantic caching strategies. ● Robustness Mindset: A track record of handling non-deterministic failures (e.g., implementing custom retry logic and fallback strategies for failed tool calls). Preferred Qualifications (The "Plus" Factors) ● Experience with Fine-tuning smaller models (e.g., Llama 3) for specific tool-calling tasks to reduce latency. ● Knowledge of Data Privacy (handling PII in agent prompts) and AI Security best practices. ● Prior experience building agents for specialized sectors like Fintech or Supply Chain.

title: AI Automation & Systems Engineer
employment_type: Full Time, Remote, Big Life Journal
description: About the Role We are a US-based, successful e-commerce and EdTech company. We are looking for a sharp, AI-native graduate who thinks in systems and solves problems with automation. This is not a traditional software engineering role — you won't spend your days writing code. Instead, you will use cutting-edge AI tools, particularly Claude Code, to design, build, and manage intelligent systems that automate our core business processes across technology, marketing, and operations. This is a rare opportunity to join at the forefront of an AI-driven transformation. If you are someone who naturally asks 'how can AI do this better?' — this role was built for you. Key Responsibilities AI Systems & Automation Design and deploy end-to-end automation workflows using Claude Code and other AI tools to eliminate manual, repetitive business processes. Build and manage AI-powered systems that integrate across our tech stack, including our membership platform, CRM, and marketing tools. Continuously identify automation opportunities across all departments and proactively propose solutions. Monitor, troubleshoot, and optimise AI-generated outputs to ensure reliability and accuracy. PHP Membership Platform Support Work alongside our development infrastructure to support and enhance our PHP-based membership system using Claude Code. Understand and interpret PHP code well enough to identify issues, request AI-generated fixes, and validate solutions without necessarily coding from scratch. Coordinate with external developers when deeper technical intervention is required. Marketing Automation Use AI tools to automate marketing workflows including email campaigns, lead nurturing, content scheduling, and funnel management. Build and maintain integrations between marketing platforms (e.g. ActiveCampaign, Mailchimp) and our core systems using AI and automation tools like Make or Zapier. Generate, optimise, and deploy AI-assisted marketing content at scale. Systems Thinking & Continuous Improvement Map out existing business processes and redesign them with an automation-first mindset. Document all systems, workflows, and automations clearly for team handover and scalability. Stay up to date with the latest developments in AI tooling and bring new ideas to the team regularly. Required Skills & Qualifications Essential Claude Code, ChatGPT, and similar platforms Strong working knowledge of AI tools, particularly Demonstrated ability to build automated workflows and systems using AI — portfolio or project examples strongly preferred. Technical literacy: ability to read, understand, and troubleshoot PHP, HTML, and basic code. Make (Integromat), Zapier, or n8n Familiarity with integration platforms such as Understanding of APIs, webhooks, and how digital systems connect. Exceptional problem-solving ability with a systems-thinking mindset. Self-driven, proactive, and comfortable working independently with minimal supervision. Preferred Experience with marketing automation platforms (ActiveCampaign, Mailchimp, HubSpot, etc.) Familiarity with CRM systems and sales funnel management. title: AI Engineer
description: Are you ready to redefine the boundaries of what's possible with Artificial Intelligence? We are searching for an exceptional AI Engineer to join our visionary team in the vibrant city of Giza, Egypt . This is an unparalleled opportunity to immerse yourself in a culture of innovation, tackle complex challenges, and contribute to groundbreaking AI solutions that will shape the future. If you are passionate about pushing technological frontiers and making a tangible impact, this role is for you! Overview: As an AI Engineer, you will be at the forefront of designing, developing, and deploying cutting-edge intelligent systems. You will work on dynamic projects, from conceptualization to execution, bringing innovative ideas to life. This on-site role offers a collaborative environment where your expertise will be pivotal in delivering high-impact AI products and services. Responsibilities: Design and implement advanced AI/ML models and algorithms, ensuring robust and scalable solutions. Conduct thorough research into new AI technologies and methodologies to continuously enhance our capabilities. Collaborate closely with cross-functional teams , including software developers, data scientists, and product managers, to integrate AI solutions seamlessly into existing systems. Develop and optimize data pipelines, perform data analysis, and pre-process large datasets to prepare them for model training. Evaluate, test, and fine-tune AI models for performance, accuracy, and efficiency. Stay abreast of industry trends, emerging technologies, and best practices in AI and machine learning. Skills & Technologies: You will leverage a powerful toolkit including AI , Machine Learning , and Deep Learning techniques. Proficiency in programming languages like Python , coupled with experience in frameworks such as TensorFlow and PyTorch , will be essential. A strong foundation in Computer Science , Information Technology (IT) , Data Science , and Natural Language Processing will be your keys to success. Growth Opportunities: We are deeply invested in your professional development. You'll gain exposure to diverse and challenging projects, enabling continuous skill enhancement and career progression. Expect opportunities for mentorship, advanced training, and the chance to lead initiatives as you grow with us. Team & Culture: Join a team of bright, passionate, and collaborative individuals who thrive on innovation and mutual support. Our culture fosters open communication, celebrates curiosity, and encourages every team member to contribute their unique perspective. We believe in collective success and a dynamic, engaging workplace. Impact: Your work as an AI Engineer will directly contribute to building intelligent systems that drive significant advancements across various sectors. You will play a crucial role in developing solutions that solve real-world problems, enhance user experiences, and contribute to our technological leadership.
requirements: Academic Excellence: A strong academic foundation in Computer Science , AI , Information Technology (IT) , or a closely related technical field is highly valued. Proven Experience: A solid track record of 1-10 years of professional experience in AI development, machine learning engineering, or a related field. Core AI Expertise: Demonstrated hands-on experience with AI , Machine Learning , and Deep Learning algorithms and methodologies. Programming Prowess: Exceptional proficiency in Python and experience with key AI/ML libraries and frameworks such as TensorFlow and PyTorch . Data Savvy: Strong understanding and practical experience in Data Science , data manipulation, and pre-processing techniques. Problem-Solving Mastery: Excellent analytical and Problem Solving skills, with the ability to design innovative solutions to complex challenges. NLP Skills: Familiarity with Natural Language Processing concepts and applications is a significant advantage. Collaboration & Communication: Ability to work effectively in a team environment and communicate complex technical concepts clearly.

title: AI Engineer (Computer Vision, Speech, NLP & Generative AI)
description: Are you passionate about building the future of AI? We are seeking a talented AI Engineer to join our innovative team. You will play a key role in optimizing and extending our AI capabilities across Computer Vision, Speech Recognition, NLP (LLMs), and Generative AI . If you thrive on deploying models in real-world production environments and driving impactful AI solutions, we want to meet you! You’ll work on enhancing our current models (YOLO, Riva) and developing new AI-powered features that fuel our products and services. Key Responsibilities Design & Develop: Build advanced machine learning and deep learning models for real-world applications. Data Exploration: Collect, analyze, and interpret large-scale datasets to extract actionable insights and identify trends. Model Optimization: Evaluate, benchmark, and optimize models for performance, scalability, and robustness. Production Deployment: Lead the deployment of AI models into production using modern frameworks and tools. Cross-functional Collaboration: Work closely with product, engineering, and business teams to integrate AI solutions seamlessly. Domain-Specific AI: Work with large construction datasets for predictive analytics and automation. LLM Engineering: Design sophisticated prompts and fine-tune large language models for optimal results. Data Visualization: Present insights using dashboards and visualization tools to stakeholders. Documentation: Test, document, and communicate model performance, efficiency, and scalability. Continuous Learning: Stay at the forefront of AI advancements, including multimodal and generative AI.
requirements: What We’re Looking For Education: Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Statistics, or related field. Experience: 3–5 years of hands-on experience developing and deploying machine learning models in production. Flexibility: Supportive, flexible working environment.

title: Senior Machine Learning Engineer
description: The problem Energy Market is the big brother to the stock market and it has similar trading mechanisms and similar forecasting problems and we are building the first AI energy trader that makes energy trading more accessible and profitable Energy prices change every few minutes. Batteries can profit from that volatility, buying low, selling high, and getting paid to stabilize the grid, but only if the trading decisions are faster and smarter than any human can manage. That’s what we’re building at Solship: an AI that trades energy autonomously, 24/7. Our models run millions of simulations per minute to decide whether a battery should charge, discharge, or hold. Every decision has direct profit and loss impact, and we’re already generating 2× revenue for pilot partners. What you’ll own • Forecasting models — design and train the models that predict energy prices, market signals across multiple markets • Real-time dispatch optimization — build the optimizer that decides battery actions every few minutes, combining ML and stochastic methods under hard physical constraints • Production ML pipeline — deploy, monitor, and improve models that execute live trades autonomously. Uptime and accuracy matter here as much as research quality. • Performance infrastructure — build the evaluation layer that tracks model performance against live market outcomes and flags degradation before it costs money • Product surface — contribute to the frontend (React/TypeScript) that makes forecasts, trades, and performance transparent to customers What you’ll get • You will be building An AI Trader nothing more cool than that — It is one of the most interesting applied ML problems out there • Equity — you’re a founding engineer, not employee #47. You’ll have real ownership in what we’re building. • Full technical ownership — you’ll define the ML architecture, choose the tools, and set the direction. No layers of approval. • Flexible, async work — we care about output, not hours logged. Work from wherever you do your best thinking. (Honest note: You will work a lot)
requirements: What we’re looking for Must-haves •      3-7 years Hands-on experience with ML-based optimization — MPC, reinforcement learning, MLIP, stochastic optimization, or similar constrained decision-making problems •      Background in time-series forecasting and control systems •      Very strong Mathematical and Modelling skills •      Strong Python engineering skills and a track record of shipping ML models to production •      Comfort operating with high autonomy in a small team where you’ll define the technical direction, not just follow it Strong advantages •      Experience with energy markets, electricity pricing, or grid operations •      Full-stack ability (React / TypeScript) for contributing directly to the product

title: Senior Data Scientist
description: We seek a highly skilled and experienced Senior Data Scientist to join our team. In this role, you will lead advanced data science solutions' design, implementation, and optimisation. You will conduct complex experiments, uncover actionable insights, develop scalable machine-learning models, and drive strategic decisions based on data. As a Senior Data Scientist, you will mentor junior team members and collaborate across departments to elevate the organisation's data-driven capabilities. Main Goals and Responsibilities: Lead the development and deployment of robust data science and machine learning pipelines, from data retrieval and preprocessing to model training, inferencing, and results visualisation; Take ownership of the architecture and optimisation of DS/ML pipelines to ensure scalability and efficiency; Leverage ML models to build innovative solutions for tasks such as text generation, content automation, and more; Collaborate with cross-functional teams to integrate advanced data solutions that meet evolving business needs; Guide tools, strategies, and best practices to solve complex data science problems, including those involving LLMs and Generative AI; Share expertise with the team and across departments to elevate the overall technical capabilities of the organisation; Proactively identify opportunities to improve data processes, model performance, and business impact. What We Offer: Long-term career stability with a competitive salary paid in USD. Conditions for steady career development. Development supported by dedicated mentors and a variety of programs focused on expertise and innovation. Private medical insurance provided after successful completion of the probationary period A well-equipped and cozy office supports comfort and productivity across all project stages. Welcoming atmosphere and a friendly corporate culture.

title: Principal OR Engineer (Optimization & Decision Intelligence)
description: As a Principal AI Architect, you will define and govern the end-to-end technical architecture of Makkook’s Optimization & Decision Intelligence platform(s), and ensure we can deliver new customer implementations fast (target: ≤ 8 weeks per use case after core platform maturity). Core Responsibilities: Platform Architecture & Technical Strategy Define platform architecture: modular components, data contracts, integration patterns, deployment topology (cloud/on-prem), and security/observability standards. Design reusable “platform primitives”: canonical entities (resources/tasks/time/cost), constraint packs, objective templates, scenario manager, and explainability layer. Make solver/stack decisions (build vs buy), and establish technical roadmaps with the CTO. Optimization & Decision Engine Leadership Lead modeling and solution strategy for key families: Production planning & scheduling (sequence-dependent setups/changeovers, minimum run lengths, maintenance windows) Workforce allocation and shift planning Routing, dispatching, and fleet assignment Inventory/replenishment and supply chain planning Set standards for model formulation, performance, scalability, and stability (runtime budgets, warm starts, decomposition, heuristics/hybrids where needed). Build a repeatable benchmarking harness and KPI framework (cost, service, risk, feasibility rate, runtime). Feasibility, Diagnostics & Explainability Own the approach to infeasibility detection and repair (constraint relaxation strategies, IIS where applicable, actionable diagnostics). Ensure decisions are explainable to operations teams (constraints hit, trade-offs, what changed vs previous plan, reason codes). Delivery & Engineering Excellence Partner with PM/Delivery to define scope, milestones, acceptance criteria, and technical risk management. Guide integrations with ERP/WMS/TMS/CMMS/SCADA and production data pipelines. Establish OptOps practices: versioning, monitoring, runtime regressions, rollback, and quality gates. Technical Leadership Mentor AI engineers (including those coming from NLP/CV backgrounds) into OR/Optimization craftsmanship. Run architecture reviews, ADRs, and enforce best practices across code, models, and deployments.
requirements: 7+ years in applied optimization / operations research / decision intelligence, with hands-on delivery experience. Strong track record in building and deploying optimization systems using one or more of: MILP / MIP , CP/CP-SAT , decomposition, heuristics/metaheuristics, hybrid strategies. Ability to translate real operations into formal models (capacities, time windows, setup/changeover, batching, minimum run lengths, resource calendars, SLAs). Solid software engineering fundamentals for production systems (clean architecture, testing, CI/CD mindset, performance profiling). Experience integrating decision engines into real enterprise environments (APIs, event-driven patterns, data pipelines, on-prem constraints). Clear communication: can explain complex trade-offs to both engineers and operators. Hands-on experience in: OR-Tools (CP-SAT) , Gurobi/CPLEX , Pyomo , JuMP , or similar. Experience with feasibility diagnostics (IIS, relaxation, reason codes, repair recommendations). Experience in building “re-optimization loops” (daily/shift updates, warm starts, rolling-horizon planning). Observability/monitoring: Prometheus/Grafana (or equivalent), structured logging, runtime regression monitoring. Preferred to have domain exposure in manufacturing planning, logistics routing, retail replenishment, or supply chain optimization.

title: Senior Data Engineer
description: Responsible for designing, building, and maintaining robust data pipelines and platforms that enable reliable data flow across the organization. This role focuses on developing scalable and high-performance data solutions, optimizing data processing, and ensuring data availability for analytics and business applications. The Senior Data Engineer will leverage expertise in modern data engineering practices, cloud technologies, and big data platforms to deliver efficient, secure, and well-governed data ecosystems. Data Architecture & Design: Develop and maintain a scalable, secure, and high-performance data architecture aligned with business needs. Ensure seamless data flow between systems, integrating structured and unstructured data sources. Define and implement best practices for data storage, processing, and security. Select and optimize data platforms, tools, and technologies for efficiency and cost-effectiveness. Data Modeling: Design and implement data models (dimensional, relational, Data Vault, NoSQL) to support operational and analytical use cases. Ensure consistency, accuracy, and integrity of data models across the organization. Continuously refine models to improve performance and adaptability to evolving business requirements. Establish and enforce data modeling standards and governance. Data Engineering and Pipeline Development: Build, optimize, and maintain automated ETL/ELT pipelines to process large-scale data. Troubleshoot, debug and upgrade existing ETL solutions. Enhance data infrastructure to support efficient storage, retrieval, and transformation. Implement automation to reduce manual processes and increase workflow efficiency. Ensure data pipelines are robust, scalable, and support real-time and batch processing. Identify data discrepancies and develop data metrics. Innovation & Technology Adoption: Stay up to date with the latest trends in data architecture, engineering, and analytics to identify and implement innovative solutions. Research and integrate cutting-edge tools and platforms to enhance data management capabilities and streamline processes. Encourage a team culture focused on continuous improvement, supporting experimentation with new technologies and methodologies. Develop and execute a technology roadmap to ensure the team is using the best tools available to meet both current and future data needs. Optimization & Performance Management: Define and enforce data governance policies, ensuring compliance with GDPR, and other regulations. Monitor and optimize database performance, query execution, and storage efficiency. Manage data quality, metadata, and lineage to ensure reliability and transparency. Implement proactive monitoring and alerting for data infrastructure health. Monitor performance and quality control plans to identify improvements. Collaboration with Cross-Functional Teams: Work closely with data scientists, business analysts, and BI teams to understand data requirements and design solutions that enable advanced analytics, reporting, and decision-making. Act as a bridge between business units and IT, ensuring that data architecture and engineering solutions meet both technical and business needs. Lead efforts to optimize data workflows and processes across departments, driving efficiency and alignment in data operations.
requirements: Educational Requirements: Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Information Systems, or a related field. Advanced degrees provide deeper technical and strategic insights, enabling the professional to drive complex data solutions and innovations at the organizational level. Special Certification or Training Required: Certified Data Management Professional (CDMP) – Preferred for expertise in data architecture and governance is preferred. Microsoft Certified: Azure Data Engineer Associate or AWS Certified Data Engineer Associate is preferred. Required Industry Experience: 3-7 years of experience in Data Engineering, and Data Modeling with a proven track record of leading large-scale enterprise data initiatives. Expertise in designing, implementing, and optimizing complex data ecosystems in hybrid environments. Strong background in data governance, security, compliance, and risk management across enterprise systems. Hands-on experience managing data integration, migration, and transformation projects in a large-scale business setting. Technological Requirements: Deep expertise in architecting, optimizing, and managing both relational (e.g., Oracle, PostgreSQL) and NoSQL (e.g., MongoDB, Cassandra) databases. Proven track record of designing and implementing scalable, enterprise-level Data Warehouse (DWH) architectures. Expert-level proficiency in writing and tuning complex SQL queries, stored procedures, functions, and triggers for high-performance data processing. Strong analytical skills related to structured or unstructured data sets. Advanced expertise in data modeling techniques, including Data Vault, Dimensional Modeling, and Entity-Relationship Modeling. Extensive experience in building and maintaining robust, scalable data pipelines (ETL/ELT) using modern technologies Expert programming skills in Python and SQL for data engineering and automation tasks, along with a strong understanding of version control (Git) Proven expertise in architecting and managing data lakes and data warehouses for big data environments. Language Requirements: Fluent in English.

title: Quality Control Engineer
employment_type: Full Time, On-site, Abou Seif Group
description: We are seeking a meticulous and analytical Quality Control (QC) Engineer to join our team. In this role, you will be responsible for maintaining the integrity of our production standards by designing, implementing, and overseeing rigorous inspection protocols. You will act as the final line of defense, ensuring that every product leaving our facility meets both internal specifications and international quality benchmarks. Beyond simple inspection, you will analyze data trends to identify systemic issues, collaborate with production teams to rectify deviations, and champion a culture of continuous improvement and technical excellence Inspection & Verification: Perform detailed inspections of raw materials, in-process components, and finished goods using precision measuring instruments and standardized testing methodologies. Trend Prediction: Analyze historical defect data to perform Predictive Maintenance and preemptive process adjustments. Process Monitoring: Implement and manage Statistical Process Control (SPC) to monitor production stability and identify potential quality drifts before they result in defects. Defect Management: Document non-conformities and lead Root Cause Analysis (RCA) sessions using tools like 5 Whys or Fishbone diagrams to eliminate recurring issues. Documentation & Reporting: Maintain comprehensive quality logs, issue Non-Conformance Reports (NCR) , and prepare weekly quality performance summaries for management. Standards Compliance: Ensure all operational processes align with ISO 9001 (or industry-specific) standards and assist in internal/external quality audits. Supplier Quality: Evaluate vendor-supplied materials and coordinate with procurement to resolve quality disputes or material inconsistencies. Continuous Improvement: Propose and implement Lean or Six Sigma initiatives to reduce scrap rates, minimize rework, and optimize resource utilization.
requirements: 3-7 Years of experience.

title: Middle Data Engineer
description: Role Summary Take the next step in your career by joining a team where you will not just build pipelines, but design scalable data platforms. We are looking for an experienced Data Engineer who combines strong technical expertise in Python and SQL with a mindset for optimization. You will work directly with clients to build modern Data Warehouses and Lakehouses using industry-standard tools like Databricks and Azure Data Factory. The Mission To build and optimize the data backbone of our clients' businesses. Your mission goes beyond moving data; you will ensure that pipelines are resilient, scalable, and economically efficient (FinOps). You will bridge the gap between infrastructure and data, taking ownership of technical delivery and helping shape the architecture of modern data platforms. The Tech Stack Core Languages: Python or Scala (Proficient/OOP), SQL (Advanced/Analytical). Compute & Storage: Databricks , Snowflake, BigQuery, Synapse, Redshift. Orchestration: Azure Data Factory (ADF) , Airflow, Dagster. Transformation: dbt (Core/Cloud), Spark/PySpark (Optimization focus). Infrastructure: Terraform (Implementation), Docker, Kubernetes (K8s). Your Responsibilities Data Platform Development: Design and implement pipelines to ingest, clean, and transform data into Data Lakes and Data Warehouses . Performance & FinOps: Actively monitor and optimize pipelines for performance and cost. Differentiate between a working query and an efficient one. Deployment & Configuration: Configure pipeline deployments and manage environment settings using IaC tools (Terraform) and CI/CD. Quality Assurance: Implement data quality checks (e.g., Great Expectations, dbt tests) and ensure data integrity. Mentorship: Communicate with stakeholders to clarify requirements and provide technical guidance/code reviews to Junior engineers. What We Offer: Long-term career stability with a competitive salary paid in USD. Conditions for steady career development. Development supported by dedicated mentors and a variety of programs focused on expertise and innovation. Private medical insurance provided after successful completion of the probationary period A well-equipped and cozy office supports comfort and productivity across all project stages. Welcoming atmosphere and a friendly corporate culture.
requirements: Your Skills Experience: 2+ years of hands-on data engineering experience (ETL/ELT). Data Architecture: Strong understanding of Data Warehousing concepts (Star/Snowflake Schema) and Data Lake principles (Medallion Architecture). Key Platforms: Proven experience with Databricks (managing clusters, notebooks), Azure Data Factory (pipelines, data flows), Snowflake. Engineering Core: Proficiency in Python (OOP, functional programming) and strong SQL skills for complex transformations. Cloud Fluency: Practical experience with at least one major cloud (AWS, GCP, Azure) and its data services (e.g., Kinesis/Lambda, Dataflow/BigQuery, Synapse). Infrastructure Awareness: Base understanding of Infrastructure as Code (Terraform) . Ability to deploy and modify infrastructure resources independently. Communication: Ability to communicate technical decisions directly to clients and the team. Nice to Have Advanced IaC: Experience setting up complex modules in Terraform or Pulumi. Lakehouse Formats: Hands-on experience with Delta Lake or Apache Iceberg features (Time Travel, Schema Evolution). NoSQL: Experience modeling data for NoSQL databases (MongoDB, DynamoDB). Certifications: Associate-level cloud certifications (e.g., Azure Data Engineer Associate, Databricks Certified Data Engineer).

title: Field Engineer
description: Are you ready to power the future? We are on the hunt for dynamic and experienced Field Engineers to join our elite team in Alexandria, Egypt . This is an unparalleled opportunity to immerse yourself in the vibrant world of Oil & Gas/Energy, where your expertise will directly contribute to critical operations. If you thrive in challenging environments, possess a keen eye for detail, and are passionate about cutting-edge engineering, then this is your moment! Overview: As a Field Engineer, you will be at the forefront of our operations, ensuring the seamless functionality and optimal performance of complex systems within the energy sector. Your work will be crucial in maintaining the integrity and efficiency of our projects, directly impacting energy production and delivery. Responsibilities: Execute comprehensive maintenance , installation, and troubleshooting of various electrical submersible pump ( ESP ) systems and related equipment. Conduct rigorous field assessments and provide expert engineering solutions to complex operational challenges. Oversee on-site projects, ensuring adherence to safety protocols, quality standards, and operational efficiency. Collaborate with cross-functional teams to optimize system performance and minimize downtime. Generate detailed technical reports and communicate findings effectively to stakeholders. Provide technical guidance and support to junior engineers and field technicians. Skills & Technologies: You will leverage your deep understanding of ESP technology , advanced engineering principles , and robust maintenance strategies . Proficiency in data analysis, troubleshooting complex electrical and mechanical systems, and adhering to strict safety regulations will be key to your success. Growth Opportunities: This role offers a clear pathway for professional advancement within our global organization. You will gain exposure to diverse projects, cutting-edge technologies, and continuous learning initiatives designed to enhance your leadership and technical capabilities. Team & Culture: Join a collaborative, high-performing team that values innovation, safety, and mutual support. Our culture fosters an environment where every voice is heard, and every contribution is celebrated. We believe in empowering our engineers to achieve their full potential. Impact: Your work will have a tangible impact on energy security and efficiency, contributing to sustainable development and operational excellence. You will be a vital part of projects that literally keep the lights on and industries running. This is an on-site position, offering you the chance to be hands-on and directly influence our success in the field.
requirements: Required Skills: Exceptional proficiency in ESP (Electrical Submersible Pump) systems and related technologies. Strong foundation in general Engineering principles , particularly within the Oil & Gas domain. Extensive experience in equipment Maintenance , diagnostic, and repair procedures. Proven ability in Oil & Gas Operations and field service execution. Advanced Troubleshooting skills for complex electrical and mechanical systems. Demonstrated experience in Project Management within a field environment. Thorough understanding and adherence to industry Safety Regulations . Excellent capabilities in Technical Reporting and documentation. Solid grasp of Electrical Systems and their integration. Proficiency in Mechanical Systems and their operational dynamics. Adept at Data Analysis to optimize performance and predict issues. Superior Client Communication and interpersonal skills. Experience Requirements: A minimum of 5-7 years of hands-on experience as a Field Engineer or in a similar role within the Oil & Gas/Energy sector. Proven track record of successfully managing and executing field projects from inception to completion. Career Level Expectations: This is an Experienced level role, seeking professionals who are ready to take on significant responsibilities, lead initiatives, and mentor emerging talent. We are looking for individuals who can hit the ground running and make an immediate impact.

title: Infrastructure Network Operations Engineer (Data Center & WAN)
description: Overview: We are seeking a highly experienced Infrastructure Network Operations Engineer to manage and operate the network foundation of a large-scale private cloud platform serving over 200 subsidiaries. The role focuses on data center networking, WAN connectivity, and routing architecture, ensuring high availability, scalability, and performance across multi-site environments. This role is critical to ensuring the stability and performance of the network backbone of a national private cloud platform. Key Responsibilities: Operate and maintain Cisco ACI fabric including Multi-Site architecture. Manage VRFs, EPGs, contracts, and tenant network segmentation. Design and operate WAN connectivity including MPLS and SD-WAN. Manage BGP routing, internet edge, and ISP connectivity. Troubleshoot complex network issues across data center and WAN environments. Ensure high availability and redundancy across primary and DR sites. Support network integration with security platforms and cloud services. Participate in DR testing and failover operations.
requirements: Experience Requirements: 7–10 years of experience in data center and WAN networking environments. Strong hands-on experience in large-scale enterprise or service provider networks is required. Technical Expertise: Cisco ACI (Multi-Site, VRF, EPG, Contracts). Routing & Switching (BGP, OSPF, MPLS). WAN & Internet Edge architecture. SD-WAN concepts and implementation. Cisco ASR or equivalent routing platforms. Network troubleshooting and performance optimization. Preferred Certifications: CCNP or CCIE (Data Center or Service Provider). Cisco ACI Specialist certification. SD-WAN certifications (Cisco or equivalent). This role is critical to ensuring the stability and performance of the network backbone of a national private cloud platform.

This is an incredible opportunity to leverage your expertise in a challenging and rewarding on-site role, driving significant impact within our organization. If you're passionate about optimizing processes, enhancing productivity, and shaping the future of manufacturing, then this is the perfect journey for you! You'll be instrumental in designing, implementing, and maintaining robust systems that ensure peak performance across our production lines. title: Information Technology Operations Manager
description: A prominent leader in the regional construction and engineering sector is seeking a mission-driven IT Operations Manager to head its technology department. This is a high-impact leadership role centered on two core pillars: Business Continuity and Operational Efficiency . You will be responsible for the end-to-end resilience of a massive, multi-site infrastructure, ensuring that technology serves as a seamless enabler for both office-based professional services and remote project sites. This role is 70% Management and 30% Technical . You will lead a large, multi-tiered technical team (comprising User Support and Systems/Network Administration) to provide high-availability services. You will be the primary architect of the firm's disaster recovery and connectivity strategies, ensuring that mission-critical engineering and enterprise platforms remain accessible 24/7, regardless of geographical or infrastructure challenges. Strategic Operations: Lead and mentor a large IT department to achieve rigorous service-level agreements (SLAs) across diverse business units and remote locations. Resilience & Continuity: Design and implement robust failover and redundancy solutions for remote site connectivity to eliminate downtime in high-stakes environments. Infrastructure Excellence: Oversee a cloud-integrated environment and a secure network architecture optimized for high-performance BIM and engineering workflows. Security & Governance: Own the cybersecurity roadmap, ensuring data integrity, rigorous backup protocols, and secure access across all company endpoints. Technical Stewardship: Oversee the IT lifecycle, from technical specification and vendor selection to the final approval of infrastructure investments and procurement.
requirements: Senior Leadership: 18+ years in IT, with significant experience in a leadership role managing large, diverse technical teams (20+ staff). Industry Expertise: Previous experience in the Construction or Engineering sector is mandatory. Candidates must understand the specific infrastructure needs of high-performance design and BIM environments. Network & Cloud Mastery: Advanced proficiency in managing  M365, firewalls, remote site connectivity solutions. Business Continuity Focus: A proven track record of maintaining high uptime and system stability in challenging or remote geographical areas. Management Style: A "Lead-by-Example" manager who possesses the technical depth to audit complex systems while focusing on high-level operational goals. Education: Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field; professional certifications (ITIL, PMP, or senior technical certifications) are preferred.

title: Electrical Tender Leader
description: Key Responsibilities 1. Tender Management Lead the full tendering cycle for Electrical projects,  etc. Review tender documents, technical specifications, and project requirements to define scope and deliverables. Develop clear tender strategies and ensure alignment with commercial, technical, and financial objectives. Conduct site visits, pre‑bid meetings, and technical discussions with clients, consultants, & Other Stakeholders. 2. Technical & Commercial Proposal Development Prepare detailed technical proposals, compliance statements, system designs, and clarifications. Coordinate with Engineering, Procurement, Construction, and Cost Control teams to gather accurate inputs. Create complete mechanical BOQs, material lists, and cost breakdowns. Finalize Technical & Commercial offers, including pricing, exclusions, alternatives, and value engineering options. 3. Cost Estimation & Vendor Coordination Develop precise mechanical cost estimates covering materials, equipment, fabrication, installation, and indirect costs. Request, evaluate, and negotiate quotations from suppliers and subcontractors. Build strong vendor networks and maintain updated price databases for mechanical equipment and systems. Identify cost‑saving opportunities and propose optimised technical solutions. 4. Risk Analysis & Value Engineering Assess technical, commercial, and contractual risks associated with the tendering manager. Recommend mitigation strategies and competitive engineering alternatives. Support management in decision‑making related to bid/no‑bid evaluation. 5. Documentation & Submission Ensure all tender documents (technical + commercial) are complete, accurate, and aligned with client formats and requirements. Manage timelines to guarantee punctual submission of all bid components. Maintain proper documentation, templates, and historical cost data for future tenders. 6. Post‑Tender Activities Provide support during contract award and handover to the project execution team. Analyze bid performance to enhance future pricing strategies and success rates.
requirements: Qualifications & Requirements Bachelor’s degree in Electrical  Engineering. 7–10+ years of experience in tendering, proposals, or estimation within Electrical, MEP, industrial, or EPC projects. rotating/static equipment, industrial utilities). Advanced knowledge of project cost structures, engineering standards, and tendering procedures. Proficiency in MS Office, AutoCAD, and tendering/estimating software. Excellent communication, negotiation, and leadership skills. Ability to manage multiple large-scale tenders under tight deadlines. Preferred Skills Experience in EPC or large industrial/Electrical projects. Strong vendor & subcontractor network in mechanical equipment and packages. Familiarity with ERP systems, costing tools, and project management principles. Knowledge of international codes (ASHRAE, NFPA, API, ASME, ISO, etc.). Key Competencies Electrical engineering expertise Cost estimation accuracy Strategic tender planning Risk assessment Vendor management Technical writing Time management Cross‑functional coordination

title: IT Manager
description: 1) Job Purpose The IT Manager is responsible for planning, managing, and optimizing the company’s information technology infrastructure and IT services. This role ensures reliable systems availability, secure operations, effective support to internal teams, and continuous improvement of IT processes. The IT Manager will lead day-to-day IT operations, coordinate with vendors, and ensure alignment with business needs and the technology strategy set by the Head of Technology. 2) Key Responsibilities A. IT Operations & Infrastructure Management Oversee the maintenance and performance of core IT infrastructure (servers, networks, endpoints, internet connectivity, Wi-Fi, telecom where applicable). Manage system uptime, capacity planning, and performance monitoring. Ensure IT environment is secure, scalable, and aligned with organizational needs. Coordinate upgrades, patching, and lifecycle management for hardware/software. B. End-User Support & Service Delivery Manage the IT helpdesk/support function (ticketing, troubleshooting, incident resolution). Ensure timely resolution of IT issues and adherence to service-level targets. Improve user experience through documentation, training, and self-service knowledge bases. Ensure proper onboarding/offboarding for employees and contractors (accounts, access, equipment, permissions). C. Security, Compliance & Risk Management Implement and maintain security controls including access management, MFA, endpoint protection, patch compliance, and backup policies. Ensure security best practices are followed across systems and users (least privilege, role-based access). Coordinate with technology leadership to maintain compliance with relevant policies and standards. Conduct or coordinate periodic security reviews, vulnerability scanning, and remediation tracking. Maintain audit readiness by ensuring logs, policies, and evidence are stored properly. D. Identity & Access Management (IAM) Manage identity lifecycle processes: provisioning, deprovisioning, and role changes. Ensure user permissions and access rights are aligned with job responsibilities. Oversee password policies, MFA enforcement, and account recovery processes. E. Systems, Applications & Integrations (as applicable) Support internal applications and tools (ERP/CRM/WMS/e-commerce tools, internal portals, etc.) within the IT scope. Coordinate with business owners and the Head of Technology for integration requirements and enhancements. Manage APIs/integration connectivity where applicable (e.g., SSO, data sync, third-party tools). F. Network & Communication Management Manage LAN/WAN/Wi-Fi setup and reliability. Oversee IP addressing, DNS, DHCP, VPN access, and firewall policies. Coordinate with internet service providers and network vendors to handle outages and SLA issues. G. Backup, Disaster Recovery & Business Continuity Ensure backups are performed successfully and regularly tested. Maintain disaster recovery plans and participate in DR drills (at least annually or per policy). Track RPO/RTO targets with leadership and ensure operational readiness. H. Vendor Management & Procurement Manage relationships with IT vendors and service providers (hardware, software, managed services). Evaluate vendor performance, negotiate renewals, and ensure SLA compliance. Support procurement planning with the Head of Technology and Finance (where relevant). Maintain asset inventory and contract documentation. I. IT Documentation & Process Improvement Maintain documentation for systems, networks, access procedures, and operational runbooks. Improve IT processes using ticketing workflows, change management, and root-cause analysis. Build standardized onboarding documentation and SOPs for key IT operations. Support internal audits by ensuring processes are measurable and traceable. J. Budgeting & Planning Assist with annual IT budget planning: software, hardware, subscriptions, and services. Track and report IT spend, forecast needs, and recommend cost-optimization opportunities. Ensure IT expenditures align with business priorities and technology roadmap. K. Team Leadership (if applicable) Lead/mentor junior IT staff and/or contractors. Assign tasks, set priorities, manage workloads, and maintain training plans. Ensure escalation practices are followed for critical incidents.
requirements: 1) Required Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or related field (or equivalent experience). Proven experience in IT management or systems/network administration (typically 7+ years total, including at least 2 years in a leadership or managerial capacity). Strong knowledge of IT infrastructure and operations in a business environment. Hands-on experience with network and endpoint troubleshooting. Demonstrated experience with IT service management (ticketing tools, incident/change/problem management). Strong understanding of cybersecurity fundamentals: IAM, endpoint security, backups, patching, logging/monitoring. 2) Preferred Qualifications (Nice to Have) Certifications such as: ITIL (or ITSM exposure) Security+ / CISSP (or equivalent) Microsoft/Azure certifications (e.g., AZ-104) or AWS/GCP basics Network certifications (e.g., CCNA/CCNP) if relevant to your environment Experience supporting ERP/CRM/WMS/e-commerce platforms and integrations. Experience with cloud infrastructure (Azure/AWS/GCP) and cloud security basics. Experience setting up DR/BCP strategies. Experience with SSO/MFA and enterprise identity systems. 3) Technical Competencies (Expected Skill Areas) Windows and/or Linux administration Networking fundamentals (DNS, DHCP, routing, firewall concepts, VPN) Endpoint management and security (EDR/AV, patch management) Backup systems and restore testing Ticketing tools / ITSM systems (e.g., Jira Service Management, ServiceNow, Zendesk, etc.) Monitoring/observability basics (system logs, alerting) Documentation and runbook creation Change management discipline 4) Soft Skills & Leadership Competencies Strong problem-solving and structured troubleshooting Clear communication with non-technical stakeholders Ability to prioritize incidents and manage escalations Ownership mindset and accountability for service reliability Process-driven approach and continuous improvement mindset Vendor negotiation and stakeholder management 5) Scope & Work Context Collaborates closely with: Head of Technology (technology direction, roadmap alignment) Operations/HR/Finance (service needs, access, payroll/finance systems support) Vendors and service providers Works with company policies related to: IT security Data privacy and handling Asset management Access control and compliance 6) Reporting & Escalation Reports progress, key metrics, incidents, and risks to the Head of Technology. Provides immediate escalation for critical service outages, security incidents, or major vendor failures.

title: Software Developer
description: We are looking for a Junior Software Developer to join our growing technology team. The ideal candidate will have a strong foundation in .NET Core , .NET Framework , and React front-end development, along with experience in SQL Server , SSIS packages , and Azure cloud services . You will work closely with senior developers to design, build, test, and deploy high-quality software applications that meet business and technical requirements. Responsibilities Develop and maintain web applications using .NET Core , .NET Framework , and React . Design, develop, and optimize SQL Server databases, stored procedures, and queries. Build and maintain SSIS (SQL Server Integration Services) packages for data extraction, transformation, and loading (ETL). Collaborate with cross-functional teams to define, design, and ship new features. Deploy and manage applications in the Azure Cloud environment. Write clean, scalable, and maintainable code following best practices and coding standards. Participate in code reviews, testing, and debugging activities. Assist in documenting software designs, processes, and system components. Stay updated on new technologies and contribute ideas for continuous improvement.

Proficiency in C# , .NET Core , and .NET Framework . Hands-on experience with React or similar front-end JavaScript frameworks. Solid understanding of SQL Server , including writing queries, stored procedures, and optimizing performance. Experience developing and managing SSIS packages . Familiarity with Azure services (VMs, SQL Database, Storage, etc.). Understanding of RESTful API design and integration. Good knowledge of object-oriented programming and software development life cycle (SDLC) principles. Excellent problem-solving, analytical, and communication skills. Eagerness to learn and adapt to new technologies and tools. title: IT Help Desk (Application Support)
description: 1. User Support & Communication Receive inquiries, complaints, and technical issues via phone, email, WhatsApp, or ticketing system. Provide first-level troubleshooting and guidance to users. Assist users with account access, and general app navigation. Ensure professional and timely responses to all users. 2. Issue Logging & Documentation Record all cases accurately in the help desk/ticketing system. Classify issues by priority and impact level. Maintain proper documentation of recurring issues and resolutions. 3. Technical Troubleshooting (Level 1 Support) Identify whether the issue is user-related, operational, or technical. Resolve basic issues such as: Login errors OTP problems Minor booking adjustments Profile updates Escalate complex technical bugs to the App Development Company. 4. Escalation & Vendor Coordination Prepare clear technical reports for escalated issues. Communicate with the App Development Company regarding system bugs or required fixes. Follow up regularly until resolution is implemented. Test and confirm fixes before closing tickets. 5. Monitoring & Reporting Monitor application performance and recurring system issues. Provide weekly/monthly reports on: Number of tickets received Issue categories Resolution time Pending escalations Suggest improvements to enhance user experience. 6. Quality & Compliance Ensure data confidentiality and compliance with company privacy policies. Maintain service-level agreements (SLAs) for response and resolution times. 7. Trainings Provide trainings for new users on how to use the app and document these trainings to be used later
requirements: Bachelor’s degree in IT, Computer Science, or related field is a must 1–3 years of experience in Help Desk / Technical Support / Customer Support is a must Experience supporting mobile applications is a must. Familiarity with ticketing systems Basic understanding of mobile app functionality and troubleshooting. Strong communication skills in Arabic and English. Required Skills Strong problem-solving ability Clear written and verbal communication Ability to handle pressure and urgent issues Attention to detail Vendor coordination skills Basic technical understanding (APIs, app bugs, system updates – advantage)

title: Human Resources Generalist
description: Human Resources Generalist Company: ExpoStudios Reports to: Chief Operating Officer Employment Type: Full-time, On-site About ExpoStudios ExpoStudios is a professional services firm operating across events technology, video production, and software development. We build and deliver for enterprise clients and regional market leaders, combining creative production with engineered systems and data-driven campaigns. As the firm scales across multiple practice areas and geographies, we are establishing a people function to support the next stage of growth. Role Summary The Human Resources Generalist will serve as the operational backbone of the people function at ExpoStudios. This is a hands-on role spanning the full employee lifecycle—from sourcing and hiring to onboarding, performance management, compensation, compliance, and culture. You will design, implement, and run systems that support three distinct talent groups: Creative professionals Software & AI engineers Commercial & marketing teams This role is suited for someone who thrives in a build-from-scratch environment . You will not inherit a mature function, you will create it. Why This Role Exists Now ExpoStudios is in an active growth phase: Expanding marketing, production, and teams Formalizing internal structures Scaling hiring velocity Establishing performance and compensation frameworks A dedicated owner is required to build and manage the people function end-to-end. Key Responsibilities 1. Talent Acquisition & Recruitment Partner with department heads on hiring plans, job descriptions, and scorecards Manage full recruitment lifecycle: Sourcing Screening Interviews Assessments Offers & negotiation Hire across: Creative (production, design, editing) Technical (engineering, AI, cloud) Business (marketing, operations, finance) Build pipelines for hard-to-fill roles Track and report hiring metrics: Time-to-hire Offer acceptance rate Pipeline health Represent the company at events, universities, and industry forums 2. Onboarding & Integration Design structured onboarding per function Coordinate: Equipment Access Documentation First-week schedules Run probation reviews and confirmation processes Conduct 30/60/90-day feedback cycles Continuously optimize onboarding experience 3. Employee Relations & Culture Act as first point of contact for employee matters Handle grievances with discretion and consistency Mediate employee-manager conflicts Investigate workplace issues in line with policy and law Run engagement surveys and action plans Lead cultural initiatives, events, and recognition programs 4. Performance Management Implement full performance cycle: Goal setting Mid-year reviews Annual evaluations Coach managers on: Feedback delivery Performance documentation Development conversations Build career paths across functions Maintain fair calibration across departments 5. Compensation & Benefits Benchmark salaries against regional market data Coordinate payroll inputs, bonuses, and commissions Manage benefits: Medical insurance Allowances Leave balances Prepare and review offer packages Maintain compensation bands and update annually 6. People Operations & Compliance Manage contracts, documentation, and employee records Oversee: Work permits Hiring process Ensure compliance with Egypt labor law and regulations Maintain and update employee handbook Implement & manage HRIS Report on: Headcount Turnover Hiring metrics Engagement 7. Learning & Development Identify skill gaps with department heads Design or source training programs Manage L&D budget and outcomes Support continuous education initiatives 8. Policy, Governance & Risk Draft and maintain policies: Code of conduct Remote work IP & confidentiality Information security Partner with legal counsel when required Maintain audit-ready documentation Identify and escalate people-related risks All applications are reviewed.
requirements: Required Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in HR, Business, Psychology, or related field 4–7 years HR experience (min. 2 years as Generalist) Strong knowledge of: Egyptian labor law Experience in full-cycle recruitment Fluent in English & Arabic Proficiency with HRIS and ATS systems Strong written communication skills Preferred Qualifications Experience in: Professional services Creative/agency environments Technology companies Exposure to multi-functional hiring HR certifications (e.g., SHRM, CIPD) Experience building HR functions from scratch Multi-country operations exposure Compensation benchmarking knowledge Who You Are You treat HR as a craft , not administration You build structured, scalable systems You handle difficult conversations with clarity and empathy You adapt across technical, creative, and executive environments You operate with high discretion You balance speed with sound judgment You take ownership of problems and solutions What We Offer Direct exposure to leadership and strategic decisions Cross-functional experience across multiple industries Competitive compensation + performance incentives Medical insurance and regional benefits Learning and certification budget High-performance, ownership-driven culture

title: Database Vendor Support
description: Employment Type: Full-time (or Senior Contractor) Reporting to: Head of Managed Services / Technical Lead Role Summary We are seeking an experienced Senior Database Administrator to lead database vendor-support activities and provide expert-level operational, performance, and architectural support across a heterogeneous environment (MongoDB, MySQL/MariaDB, Microsoft SQL Server, and others). This hands-on role is responsible for resolving critical tickets, driving stability and performance improvements, coordinating vendor escalations, and mentoring junior DBAs. The Senior DBA will be a key escalation point for complex incidents and lead database-related projects including migrations, HA designs, and automation. Key Responsibilities Serve as primary escalation engineer for complex database incidents and SLA breaches; lead incident response and post-incident root-cause analysis (RCA). Troubleshoot and resolve high-severity tickets across MongoDB, MySQL/MariaDB, MS SQL Server, and other DBMS; coordinate cross-team and vendor escalations as required. Design, implement and maintain high-availability, replication, clustering, sharding, and disaster recovery solutions; lead DR planning and periodic DR tests. Lead performance tuning efforts: query optimization, indexing strategy, resource contention mitigation, and capacity planning for large-scale production systems. Architect and execute database migrations and upgrades (on-prem ↔ cloud, cloud-to-cloud, major version upgrades), ensuring minimal downtime and reliable rollback plans. Define and enforce database change control processes; review and approve schema changes and major configuration adjustments. Implement and maintain robust backup/restore strategies (including point-in-time recovery) and ensure recoverability through regular restore testing. Develop and maintain database security best practices: roles and permissions, encryption at rest/in transit, auditing, and compliance controls. Build and improve observability and monitoring (alerts, dashboards, anomaly detection) using tools such as Prometheus, Grafana, Datadog, New Relic, Percona Monitoring, or vendor solutions. Automate repetitive operational tasks and integrate database operations into CI/CD pipelines using scripting (Python, Bash) and IaC tools (Ansible, Terraform). Collaborate with application, infrastructure, and security teams to design scalable data architectures that meet performance, reliability, and compliance requirements. Maintain thorough documentation: runbooks, SOPs, capacity forecasts, architecture diagrams, and vendor escalation procedures. Mentor and coach junior DBAs and support engineers; lead knowledge transfer and run regular training sessions. Participate in on-call rotation for critical incidents and provide after-hours support when required.
requirements: Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Information Systems, or equivalent experience. 4+ years of hands-on DBA experience with strong, demonstrable expertise in at least two of: MongoDB, MySQL/MariaDB, Microsoft SQL Server. Proven track record of resolving complex production incidents and driving long-term improvements. Deep SQL expertise and experience optimizing complex queries, indexing strategies, and execution plans; familiarity with MongoDB query optimization and sharding strategies. Strong experience with HA, replication, clustering, sharding, and disaster recovery design and operations. Hands-on experience with cloud-managed database services (AWS RDS/Aurora, Azure SQL, Google Cloud SQL, MongoDB Atlas) and hybrid cloud deployments. Experience with database migration projects, schema evolution strategies, and zero-downtime deployment techniques. Proficiency with monitoring/observability tools and log analysis; strong diagnostic skills for performance and resource-related issues. Scripting/automation skills (Python, Bash, PowerShell) and experience with configuration management / IaC (Ansible, Terraform, CI/CD tooling). Solid understanding of Linux and Windows server environments, storage, and networking as they relate to database performance. Excellent communication skills; comfortable coordinating with vendors (MongoDB, Microsoft, Oracle, etc.) and presenting technical information to stakeholders. Strong documentation skills and commitment to operational excellence and compliance. English proficiency required for vendor interaction and technical documentation. Preferred / Nice-to-have Certifications: MongoDB DBA, Microsoft Certified: Azure Database Administrator, MySQL/MariaDB certifications, or similar. Experience with containerized databases and Kubernetes operators (e.g., MongoDB Kubernetes Operator, MySQL Operator). Familiarity with observability platforms (ELK/EFK stacks), query profilers, and advanced performance tools (Query Store, EXPLAIN ANALYZE). Prior experience working with global enterprise customers, RFP-managed services, or partner ecosystems (e.g., Dell Technologies). Experience with security and compliance frameworks (ISO 27001, SOC2, PCI-DSS). Deliverables & KPIs SLA adherence and mean time to resolution (MTTR) for high-severity incidents. Reduction in recurring incidents (trend-based KPIs) after RCA implementations. Successful execution of migrations/upgrades with minimal downtime. Availability and reliability metrics for database platforms (uptime, replication lag). Timeliness and quality of runbooks, documentation, and knowledge transfer sessions.
